---
title: "03 Localization State Estimation"
format: html
execute:
  eval: false
---

## Main Question

How can systems estimate their state using noisy observations?

## Runnable Example

```python
from ipcv.evaluation import evaluate_trajectory
from ipcv.localization import (
    KalmanLocalizationEstimator,
    LocalizationConfig,
    generate_ground_truth,
    simulate_observations,
)

config = LocalizationConfig(steps=25)
truth = generate_ground_truth(config)
observations = simulate_observations(truth, config)
estimates = KalmanLocalizationEstimator(config).run(observations)
result = evaluate_trajectory(truth, estimates)
```

## Engineering Focus

The estimator is isolated from data loading, failure injection, visualization, and MLflow. That separation makes the math easier to test and the system easier to change.

## Theory Submodule

Use [Probability, Statistics, And State Estimation](03a_probability_statistics_state_estimation.qmd) for the deeper Kalman and Bayesian filtering background. That section links selected notebooks from the external Kalman book submodule and connects them back to this course's localization estimator.

## Exercise

Change observation noise and compare `trajectory_rmse`, `final_drift`, and `mean_innovation_norm`.
